content:
I Let an AI Red-Team My FastAPI App. It Found 7 Vulnerabilities in 12 Minutes — And I Built Them All

10 min read
Just now

--

How I went from “my code is fine” to rotating every API key I own before lunch
I used to think AI security tools were overhyped.
I’d read the blog posts. I’d seen the LinkedIn influencers post screenshots of ChatGPT finding “critical vulnerabilities” that turned out to be missing semicolons. I was a real developer. I wrote real code. I had Pydantic models, dependency injection, and a README.md that actually explained how to run the project. I was fine.
Then last Thursday, I pasted my FastAPI repo into an AI red-teaming tool, made a cup of coffee, and came back to a report that made me want to delete my GitHub account.
Twelve minutes.
Seven vulnerabilities.
And every single one of them was my fault.
The Setup: Hubris, Staging, and a False Sense of Safety
I’ve been building this FastAPI app for eight months. It started as a side project — a task management API with team collaboration features. Now it has 4,000 users, real subscriptions through Stripe, and a PostgreSQL database full of actual human data that I’m legally responsible for protecting.
I thought I was being careful.
I used python-jose for JWTs. I had rate limiting with SlowAPI. My Docker containers ran as non-root users…
